title: selliguea taeniata var. borneensis
intro: Selliguea taeniata var. borneensis, also known as the Bornean polypody, is a type of fern native to the island of Borneo. This plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Selliguea taeniata var. borneensis include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat various respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, it is often combined with other plants to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and effects on human health.
